records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests for Manumission, Charity, and Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5497 - Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr Al-Bazanti narrated from Ahmad ibn Ziyad who said: I asked Abu Al-Hasan (as) about a man who was on his deathbed and had slaves solely for himself and others jointly owned with another person. In his will, he stated, "My slaves are free, except for those who are in partnership."
Imam (as) wrote in response: "They should be appraised against his estate, and if his wealth can cover it, then they are free."
records.arabicText: 5497 - وَ رَوَى أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ اَلْبَزَنْطِيُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ زِيَادٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ تَحْضُرُهُ اَلْوَفَاةُ وَ لَهُ مَمَالِيكُ لِخَاصَّةِ نَفْسِهِ وَ مَمَالِيكُ فِي اَلشِّرْكَةِ مَعَ رَجُلٍ آخَرَ فَيُوصِي فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ مَمَالِيكِي أَحْرَارٌ مَا خَلاَ مَمَالِيكِيَ اَلَّذِينَ فِي اَلشِّرْكَةِ فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "يُقَوَّمُونَ عَلَيْهِ إِنْ كَانَ مَالُهُ يَحْتَمِلُ ثُمَّ هُمْ أَحْرَارٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5497 - Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr Al-Bazanti narrated from Ahmad ibn Ziyad who said: I asked Abu Al-Hasan (as) about a man who was on his deathbed and had slaves solely for himself and others jointly owned with another person. In his will, he stated,
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"My slaves are free, except for those who are in partnership."
Imam (as) wrote in response: "They should be appraised against his estate, and if his wealth can cover it, then they are free."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests for Manumission, Charity, and Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5498 - Muhammad ibn Ismaʿil ibn Baziʿ narrated from Ali ibn Al-Nuʿman, from Suwayd Al-Qallaʾ, from Ayyub ibn Al-Hurr, from Abu Bakr Al-Haḍrami, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as), "Alqamah ibn Muhammad instructed me in his will to free a slave on his behalf, so I freed a female slave for him. Is that sufficient, or should I free another from my own wealth?"
Imam (as) replied: "It suffices him." Then Imam (as) added, "Indeed, Faṭimah, the mother of my son, had instructed me to free a slave on her behalf, so I freed a female slave for her."
records.arabicText: 5498 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ بَزِيعٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلنُّعْمَانِ عَنْ سُوَيْدٍ اَلْقَلاَّءِ عَنْ أَيُّوبَ بْنِ اَلْحُرِّ عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرٍ اَلْحَضْرَمِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّ عَلْقَمَةَ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ أَوْصَى أَنْ أُعْتِقَ عَنْهُ رَقَبَةً فَأَعْتَقْتُ عَنْهُ اِمْرَأَةً أَ فَتُجْزِيهِ أَوْ أُعْتِقُ عَنْهُ مِنْ مَالِي قَالَ "يُجْزِيهِ" ثُمَّ قَالَ "إِنَّ فَاطِمَةَ أُمَّ اِبْنِي أَوْصَتْ أَنْ أُعْتِقَ عَنْهَا رَقَبَةً فَأَعْتَقْتُ عَنْهَا اِمْرَأَةً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5498 - Muhammad ibn Ismaʿil ibn Baziʿ narrated from Ali ibn Al-Nuʿman, from Suwayd Al-Qallaʾ, from Ayyub ibn Al-Hurr, from Abu Bakr Al-Haḍrami, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as), "Alqamah ibn Muhammad instructed me in his will to free a slave on his behalf, so I freed a female slave for him. Is that sufficient, or should I free another from my own wealth?"
Imam (as) replied: "It suffices him." Then Imam (as) added, "Indeed, Faṭimah, the mother of my son, had instructed me to free a slave on her behalf, so I freed a female slave for her."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests for Manumission, Charity, and Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5499 - Muawiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who died and had instructed that Hajj be performed on his behalf. Imam (as) replied: "If he had never performed Hajj (was a ṣarurah), then Hajj should be performed on his behalf from the middle of his wealth. But if he was not a ṣarurah, then it should be performed from one-third of his wealth."
records.arabicText: 5499 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مَاتَ وَ أَوْصَى أَنْ يُحَجَّ عَنْهُ قَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ صَرُورَةً حُجَّ عَنْهُ مِنْ وَسَطِ اَلْمَالِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ غَيْرَ صَرُورَةٍ فَمِنَ اَلثُّلُثِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5499 - Muawiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who died and had instructed that Hajj be performed on his behalf. Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If he had never performed Hajj (was a ṣarurah), then Hajj should be performed on his behalf from the middle of his wealth. But if he was not a ṣarurah, then it should be performed from one-third of his wealth."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests for Manumission, Charity, and Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5500 - And Imam (as) said regarding a woman who bequeathed her wealth for emancipation (freeing slaves), performing Hajj, and giving charity, but her wealth was insufficient: "Begin with Hajj because it is an obligation. If anything remains, allocate a portion for charity and a portion for emancipation."
records.arabicText: 5500 - وَ قَالَ : فِي اِمْرَأَةٍ أَوْصَتْ بِمَالٍ فِي عِتْقٍ وَ حَجٍّ وَ صَدَقَةٍ فَلَمْ يَبْلُغْ قَالَ "اِبْدَأْ بِالْحَجِّ فَإِنَّهُ مَفْرُوضٌ فَإِنْ بَقِيَ شَيْءٌ فَاجْعَلْ فِي اَلصَّدَقَةِ طَائِفَةً وَ فِي اَلْعِتْقِ طَائِفَةً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5500 - And Imam (as) said regarding a woman who bequeathed her wealth for emancipation (freeing slaves), performing Hajj, and giving charity, but her wealth was insufficient:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Begin with Hajj because it is an obligation. If anything remains, allocate a portion for charity and a portion for emancipation."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests for Manumission, Charity, and Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5501 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah who said:
I asked Abu Al-Hasan (as) about a man who bequeathed thirty dinar for the emancipation of a man from among our companions, but no such person was found.
Imam (as) said: "A person should be purchased from among the people and then emancipated."
records.arabicText: 5501 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى بِثَلاَثِينَ دِينَاراً يُعْتَقُ بِهَا رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا فَلَمْ يُوجَدْ بِذَلِكَ قَالَ "يُشْتَرَى مِنَ اَلنَّاسِ فَيُعْتَقُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5501 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah who said:
I asked Abu Al-Hasan (as) about a man who bequeathed thirty dinar for the emancipation of a man from among our companions, but no such person was found.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A person should be purchased from among the people and then emancipated."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests for Manumission, Charity, and Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5502 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Imam (as) that he said: "Then let them purchase from among the general people as long as he is not a Naṣibi (one who harbors enmity towards the Ahl Al-Bayt (as))."
records.arabicText: 5502 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْهُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "فَلْيَشْتَرُوا مِنْ عُرْضِ اَلنَّاسِ مَا لَمْ يَكُنْ نَاصِبِيّاً ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5502 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Imam (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Then let them purchase from among the general people as long as he is not a Naṣibi (one who harbors enmity towards the Ahl Al-Bayt (as))."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests for Manumission, Charity, and Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5503 - Aban ibn Uthman narrated from Muhammad ibn Marwan from the Shaykh, meaning Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), from his father, Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), peace be upon them both, that Imam (as) said:
"Indeed, Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), passed away and left sixty servants. He freed one-third of them, then I drew lots among the remaining ones and freed another one-third."
records.arabicText: 5503 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانُ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مَرْوَانَ عَنِ اَلشَّيْخِ يَعْنِي مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "إِنَّ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَاتَ وَ تَرَكَ سِتِّينَ مَمْلُوكاً فَأَعْتَقَ ثُلُثَهُمْ فَأَقْرَعْتُ بَيْنَهُمْ وَ أَعْتَقْتُ اَلثُّلُثَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5503 - Aban ibn Uthman narrated from Muhammad ibn Marwan from the Shaykh, meaning Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), from his father, Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), peace be upon them both, that Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), passed away and left sixty servants. He freed one-third of them, then I drew lots among the remaining ones and freed another one-third."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests for Manumission, Charity, and Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5504 - Al-Qasim Muhammad Al-Jawhari narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah from Abu Basir, who said: I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), about a freed woman whom my brother had emancipated. She used to serve the maidservants and was living among his family. He had instructed me to spend on her moderately.
Abu Jafar (as), said: "If she remained with the maidservants and continued serving them, then spend on her and follow his will."
records.arabicText: 5504 - وَ رَوَى اَلْقَاسِمُ مُحَمَّدٌ اَلْجَوْهَرِيُّ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ مُحَرَّرَةٍ كَانَ أَعْتَقَهَا أَخِي وَ قَدْ كَانَتْ تَخْدُمُ اَلْجَوَارِيَ وَ كَانَتْ فِي عِيَالِهِ فَأَوْصَانِي أَنْ أُنْفِقَ عَلَيْهَا مِنَ اَلْوَسَطِ فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَتْ مَعَ اَلْجَوَارِي وَ أَقَامَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ فَأَنْفِقْ عَلَيْهَا وَ اِتَّبِعْ وَصِيَّتَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5504 - Al-Qasim Muhammad Al-Jawhari narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah from Abu Basir, who said: I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), about a freed woman whom my brother had emancipated. She used to serve the maidservants and was living among his family. He had instructed me to spend on her moderately.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Abu Jafar (as), said: "If she remained with the maidservants and continued serving them, then spend on her and follow his will."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests for Manumission, Charity, and Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5505 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Ayyub from Sama'ah, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who had made a will that a soul be freed on his behalf from one-third of his wealth with five hundred dirhams. The executor of the will bought a soul for less than five hundred, and some amount remained. What do you say about the remaining amount?
Imam (as) said: "It should be given to the purchased soul before it is freed, and then it should be freed on behalf of the deceased."
records.arabicText: 5505 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى أَنْ يُعْتَقَ عَنْهُ نَسَمَةٌ مِنْ ثُلُثِهِ بِخَمْسِمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ فَاشْتَرَى اَلْوَصِيُّ نَسَمَةً بِأَقَلَّ مِنْ خَمْسِمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ وَ فَضَلَتْ فَضْلَةٌ فَمَا تَرَى فِي اَلْفَضْلَةِ قَالَ "تُدْفَعُ إِلَى اَلنَّسَمَةِ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تُعْتَقَ ثُمَّ تُعْتَقُ عَنِ اَلْمَيِّتِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5505 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Ayyub from Sama'ah, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who had made a will that a soul be freed on his behalf from one-third of his wealth with five hundred dirhams. The executor of the will bought a soul for less than five hundred, and some amount remained. What do you say about the remaining amount?
Imam (as) said: "It should be given to the purchased soul before it is freed, and then it should be freed on behalf of the deceased."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests for the Mukatab and Umm Al-Walad
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5506 - Asim ibn Humayd narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
"The Commander of the Faithful (as), peace be upon him, ruled concerning a contracted slave (mukatab) who was married to a free woman. She made a bequest for him at the time of her death.
Imam (as) said: 'The people entitled to inheritance cannot have her will executed for him because he is a mukatab who has not been freed.'
Then Imam (as), peace be upon him, ruled that he would inherit in proportion to what had been freed of him and that the will for him would be valid in proportion to what had been freed of him."
Imam (as) also ruled regarding a mukatab for whom a will had been made, and he had paid half of what was due upon him, that half of the will was valid for him.
Imam (as) also ruled regarding a mukatab who had paid a quarter of what was due upon him and for whom a will had been made, that a quarter of the will was valid for him.
Imam (as) said about a man who made a will for his female mukatabah and she had paid one-sixth of what was due upon her, that the will was valid for her in proportion to what had been freed of her.
records.arabicText: 5506 - رَوَى عَاصِمُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي مُكَاتَبٍ كَانَتْ تَحْتَهُ اِمْرَأَةٌ حُرَّةٌ فَأَوْصَتْ لَهُ عِنْدَ مَوْتِهَا بِوَصِيَّةٍ فَقَالَ "أَهْلُ اَلْمِيرَاثِ لاَ تَجُوزُ وَصِيَّتُهَا لَهُ إِنَّهُ مُكَاتَبٌ لَمْ يُعْتَقْ" فَقَضَى عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "أَنَّهُ يَرِثُ بِحِسَابِ مَا أُعْتِقَ مِنْهُ وَ يَجُوزُ لَهُ مِنَ اَلْوَصِيَّةِ بِحِسَابِ مَا أُعْتِقَ مِنْهُ" وَ قَضَى عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي مُكَاتَبٍ أُوصِيَ لَهُ بِوَصِيَّةٍ وَ قَدْ قَضَى نِصْفَ مَا عَلَيْهِ فَأَجَازَ لَهُ نِصْفَ اَلْوَصِيَّةِ وَ قَضَى فِي مُكَاتَبٍ قَضَى رُبُعَ مَا عَلَيْهِ فَأُوصِيَ لَهُ بِوَصِيَّةٍ فَأَجَازَ لَهُ رُبُعَ اَلْوَصِيَّةِ وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى لِمُكَاتَبَتِهِ وَ قَدْ قَضَتْ سُدُسَ مَا كَانَ عَلَيْهَا فَأَجَازَ لَهَا بِحِسَابِ مَا أُعْتِقَ مِنْهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5506 - Asim ibn Humayd narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The Commander of the Faithful (as), peace be upon him, ruled concerning a contracted slave (mukatab) who was married to a free woman. She made a bequest for him at the time of her death.
Imam (as) said: 'The people entitled to inheritance cannot have her will executed for him because he is a mukatab who has not been freed.'
Then Imam (as), peace be upon him, ruled that he would inherit in proportion to what had been freed of him and that the will for him would be valid in proportion to what had been freed of him."
Imam (as) also ruled regarding a mukatab for whom a will had been made, and he had paid half of what was due upon him, that half of the will was valid for him.
Imam (as) also ruled regarding a mukatab who had paid a quarter of what was due upon him and for whom a will had been made, that a quarter of the will was valid for him.
Imam (as) said about a man who made a will for his female mukatabah and she had paid one-sixth of what was due upon her, that the will was valid for her in proportion to what had been freed of her.

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests for the Mukatab and Umm Al-Walad
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5507 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Jamil ibn Salih from Abu Ubaydah, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who had a slave woman with whom he had a son. When death approached him, he bequeathed for her two thousand dirhams or more and instructed his heirs to enslave her.
Imam (as) said: "No, rather she should be freed from one-third of the deceased's estate and given what he had bequeathed for her."
records.arabicText: 5507 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ جَمِيلِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ كَانَتْ لَهُ أُمُّ وَلَدٍ وَ لَهُ مِنْهَا غُلاَمٌ فَلَمَّا حَضَرَتْهُ اَلْوَفَاةُ أَوْصَى لَهَا بِأَلْفَيْ دِرْهَمٍ أَوْ بِأَكْثَرَ لِلْوَرَثَةِ أَنْ يَسْتَرِقُّوهَا فَقَالَ "لاَ بَلْ تُعْتَقُ مِنْ ثُلُثِ اَلْمَيِّتِ وَ تُعْطَى مَا أَوْصَى لَهَا بِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5507 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Jamil ibn Salih from Abu Ubaydah, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who had a slave woman with whom he had a son. When death approached him, he bequeathed for her two thousand dirhams or more and instructed his heirs to enslave her.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "No, rather she should be freed from one-third of the deceased's estate and given what he had bequeathed for her."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests for the Mukatab and Umm Al-Walad
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5508 - It was narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr Al-Bazanti, who said:
I copied from a book in the handwriting of Abu Al-Hasan (as):
"A certain person, your master, passed away, leaving behind a paternal nephew who also passed away. The nephew left behind an umm walad (a concubine who bore him no children) and bequeathed to her a thousand dirhams. Does the bequest hold validity, and does she receive emancipation? What is her status? May I be your ransom, I seek your opinion on this matter."
Imam (as) wrote back: "She is to be freed from one-third (of the estate), and she has the bequest."
records.arabicText: 5508 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ اَلْبَزَنْطِيِّ قَالَ : نَسَخْتُ مِنْ كِتَابٍ بِخَطِّ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فُلاَنٌ مَوْلاَكَ تُوُفِّيَ اِبْنُ أَخٍ لَهُ فَتَرَكَ أُمَّ وَلَدٍ لَهُ لَيْسَ لَهَا وَلَدٌ وَ أَوْصَى لَهَا بِأَلْفِ دِرْهَمٍ هَلْ تَجُوزُ اَلْوَصِيَّةُ وَ هَلْ يَقَعُ عَلَيْهَا عِتْقٌ وَ مَا حَالُهَا رَأْيَكَ فَدَتْكَ نَفْسِي فِي ذَلِكَ فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "تُعْتَقُ مِنَ اَلثُّلُثِ وَ لَهَا اَلْوَصِيَّةُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5508 - It was narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr Al-Bazanti, who said:
I copied from a book in the handwriting of Abu Al-Hasan (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"A certain person, your master, passed away, leaving behind a paternal nephew who also passed away. The nephew left behind an umm walad (a concubine who bore him no children) and bequeathed to her a thousand dirhams. Does the bequest hold validity, and does she receive emancipation? What is her status? May I be your ransom, I seek your opinion on this matter."
Imam (as) wrote back: "She is to be freed from one-third (of the estate), and she has the bequest."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Making a Bequest of a Sword, a Chest, or a Ship to Another Man
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5509 - Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr narrated from Abu Jamilah from Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as), who said:
I asked him about a man who bequeathed a sword to another man, and the sword was in its sheath and adorned with decorations. The heirs said to him, "Only the blade belongs to you, not the sword (with its accessories)."
Imam (as) said: "No, rather the sword with everything it contains belongs to him."
I said to Imam (as): "A man bequeathed a box to another man, and there was money inside it. The heirs said: 'Only the box belongs to you, not the money.'"
Imam (as) said: "The box with everything it contains belongs to him."
records.arabicText: 5509 - رَوَى أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَمِيلَةَ عَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى لِرَجُلٍ بِسَيْفٍ وَ كَانَ فِي جَفْنٍ وَ عَلَيْهِ حِلْيَةٌ فَقَالَ لَهُ اَلْوَرَثَةُ إِنَّمَا لَكَ اَلنَّصْلُ وَ لَيْسَ لَكَ اَلسَّيْفُ فَقَالَ "لاَ بَلِ اَلسَّيْفُ بِمَا فِيهِ لَهُ" قَالَ قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ أَوْصَى بِصُنْدُوقٍ لِرَجُلٍ وَ كَانَ فِيهِ مَالٌ فَقَالَ اَلْوَرَثَةُ إِنَّمَا لَكَ اَلصُّنْدُوقُ وَ لَيْسَ لَكَ اَلْمَالُ فَقَالَ "اَلصُّنْدُوقُ بِمَا فِيهِ لَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5509 - Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr narrated from Abu Jamilah from Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked him about a man who bequeathed a sword to another man, and the sword was in its sheath and adorned with decorations. The heirs said to him, "Only the blade belongs to you, not the sword (with its accessories)."
Imam (as) said: "No, rather the sword with everything it contains belongs to him."
I said to Imam (as): "A man bequeathed a box to another man, and there was money inside it. The heirs said: 'Only the box belongs to you, not the money.'"
Imam (as) said: "The box with everything it contains belongs to him."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Making a Bequest of a Sword, a Chest, or a Ship to Another Man
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5510 - Muhammad ibn Al-Husayn narrated from Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Hilal from Uqbah ibn Khalid from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about a man who said:
"This ship belongs to so-and-so," without specifying what was inside it, and there was food in it. Should the man be given the ship and what is inside it?
Imam (as) said: "It belongs to the one to whom it was bequeathed, unless its owner explicitly excluded what was inside it. The heirs have no claim over it."
records.arabicText: 5510 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ هِلاَلٍ عَنْ عُقْبَةَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَالَ هَذِهِ اَلسَّفِينَةُ لِفُلاَنٍ وَ لَمْ يُسَمِّ مَا فِيهَا وَ فِيهَا طَعَامٌ أَ يُعْطَاهَا اَلرَّجُلُ وَ مَا فِيهَا قَالَ "هِيَ لِلَّذِي أَوْصَى لَهُ بِهَا إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ صَاحِبُهَا اِسْتَثْنَى مَا فِيهَا وَ لَيْسَ لِلْوَرَثَةِ شَيْءٌ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5510 - Muhammad ibn Al-Husayn narrated from Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Hilal from Uqbah ibn Khalid from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about a man who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"This ship belongs to so-and-so," without specifying what was inside it, and there was food in it. Should the man be given the ship and what is inside it?
Imam (as) said: "It belongs to the one to whom it was bequeathed, unless its owner explicitly excluded what was inside it. The heirs have no claim over it."

records.chapter: Chapter on One Who Does Not Make a Bequest While Having Heirs, if His Estate Divided Among Them or Sold for Their Benefit
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5511 - Zur'ah narrated from Sama'ah, who said: I asked Imam (as) about a man who died and left behind sons and daughters, both young and old, without leaving a will. He also had servants, slaves, and jewelry. How should the heirs divide that inheritance?
Imam (as) said: "If a trustworthy man undertakes the division of all that among them, then there is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 5511 - رَوَى زُرْعَةُ عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مَاتَ وَ لَهُ بَنُونَ وَ بَنَاتٌ صِغَارٌ وَ كِبَارٌ مِنْ غَيْرِ وَصِيَّةٍ وَ لَهُ خَدَمٌ وَ مَمَالِيكُ وَ عُقَدٌ كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ اَلْوَرَثَةُ بِقِسْمَةِ ذَلِكَ اَلْمِيرَاثِ قَالَ "إِنْ قَامَ رَجُلٌ ثِقَةٌ قَاسَمَهُمْ ذَلِكَ كُلَّهُ فَلاَ بَأْسَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5511 - Zur'ah narrated from Sama'ah, who said: I asked Imam (as) about a man who died and left behind sons and daughters, both young and old, without leaving a will. He also had servants, slaves, and jewelry. How should the heirs divide that inheritance?
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If a trustworthy man undertakes the division of all that among them, then there is no harm in it."

records.chapter: Chapter on One Who Does Not Make a Bequest While Having Heirs, if His Estate Divided Among Them or Sold for Their Benefit
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5512 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab, who said:
I asked Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), about a man related to me who died and left behind young children and male and female slaves. He did not leave a will.
What is your opinion regarding someone who buys a female slave from them and takes her as an umm walad (a concubine who bears a child)? And what is your opinion on selling them?
Imam (as) said: "If they have a guardian who manages their affairs, sells on their behalf, and looks after their interests, then he will be rewarded for it."
I said: "What do you say about someone who buys a female slave from them and takes her as an umm walad?"
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in that if the guardian responsible for their welfare sells to him, considering what is in their best interest. They have no right to revoke what the guardian, acting in their best interest, has done."
records.arabicText:
5512 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ قَالَ :
سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ بَيْنِي وَ بَيْنَهُ قَرَابَةٌ مَاتَ وَ تَرَكَ أَوْلاَداً صِغَاراً وَ تَرَكَ مَمَالِيكَ لَهُ غِلْمَاناً وَ جَوَارِيَ وَ لَمْ يُوصِ فَمَا تَرَى فِيمَنْ يَشْتَرِي مِنْهُمُ اَلْجَارِيَةَ فَيَتَّخِذُهَا أُمَّ وَلَدٍ وَ مَا تَرَى فِي بَيْعِهِمْ فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ لَهُمْ وَلِيٌّ يَقُومُ بِأَمْرِهِمْ بَاعَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَ نَظَرَ لَهُمْ كَانَ مَأْجُوراً فِيهِمْ" قُلْتُ فَمَا تَرَى فِيمَنْ يَشْتَرِي مِنْهُمُ اَلْجَارِيَةَ فَيَتَّخِذُهَا أُمَّ وَلَدٍ قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ إِذَا بَاعَ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلْقَيِّمُ لَهُمْ اَلنَّاظِرُ فِيمَا يُصْلِحُهُمْ وَ لَيْسَ لَهُمْ أَنْ يَرْجِعُوا عَمَّا صَنَعَ اَلْقَيِّمُ لَهُمُ اَلنَّاظِرُ فِيمَا يُصْلِحُهُمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5512 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), about a man related to me who died and left behind young children and male and female slaves. He did not leave a will.
What is your opinion regarding someone who buys a female slave from them and takes her as an umm walad (a concubine who bears a child)? And what is your opinion on selling them?
Imam (as) said: "If they have a guardian who manages their affairs, sells on their behalf, and looks after their interests, then he will be rewarded for it."
I said: "What do you say about someone who buys a female slave from them and takes her as an umm walad?"
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in that if the guardian responsible for their welfare sells to him, considering what is in their best interest. They have no right to revoke what the guardian, acting in their best interest, has done."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Who Makes a Bequest, but the Executor Forgets it and Only Remembers One Part of it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5513 - Muhammad ibn Al-Hasan Al-Saffar, may Allah (swt) be pleased with him, narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Muhammad ibn Rayyan, who said:
I wrote to Imam (as) - meaning Imam Ali ibn Muhammad Al-Hadi (as) - asking about a person who had made a will, but the executor only remembered one part of it. What should be done with the remaining parts?
Imam (as) replied: "Allocate the remaining parts to charitable acts."
records.arabicText: 5513 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ اَلْحَسَنِ اَلصَّفَّارُ رَضِيَ اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ رَيَّانَ قَالَ : كَتَبْتُ إِلَيْهِ يَعْنِي عَلِيَّ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَسْأَلُهُ عَنْ إِنْسَانٍ أَوْصَى بِوَصِيَّةٍ فَلَمْ يَحْفَظِ اَلْوَصِيُّ إِلاَّ بَاباً وَاحِداً مِنْهَا كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ فِي اَلْبَاقِي فَوَقَّعَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "اَلْأَبْوَابَ اَلْبَاقِيَةَ اِجْعَلْهَا فِي اَلْبِرِّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5513 - Muhammad ibn Al-Hasan Al-Saffar, may Allah (swt) be pleased with him, narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Muhammad ibn Rayyan, who said:
I wrote to Imam (as) - meaning Imam Ali ibn Muhammad Al-Hadi (as) - asking about a person who had made a will, but the executor only remembered one part of it. What should be done with the remaining parts?
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Allocate the remaining parts to charitable acts."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Executor Purchasing From the deceased's Estate When it is Sold to the Highest Bidder
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5514 - Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Yahya narrated from Al-Husayn ibn Ibrahim Al-Hamdani, who said: I wrote along with Muhammad ibn Yahya asking whether the executor of a will is permitted to purchase something from the deceased's estate when it is sold to the highest bidder and take it for himself.
Imam (as) replied: "It is permissible if he purchases it properly."
records.arabicText: 5514 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ اَلْهَمْدَانِيِّ قَالَ : كَتَبْتُ مَعَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى هَلْ لِلْوَصِيِّ أَنْ يَشْتَرِيَ شَيْئاً مِنْ مَالِ اَلْمَيِّتِ إِذَا بِيعَ فِيمَنْ زَادَ يَزِيدُ وَ يَأْخُذُ لِنَفْسِهِ فَقَالَ "يَجُوزُ إِذَا اِشْتَرَى صَحِيحاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5514 - Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Yahya narrated from Al-Husayn ibn Ibrahim Al-Hamdani, who said: I wrote along with Muhammad ibn Yahya asking whether the executor of a will is permitted to purchase something from the deceased's estate when it is sold to the highest bidder and take it for himself.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is permissible if he purchases it properly."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Disinheriting His Son for Having Relations with His father's Umm Al-Walad
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5515 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali Al-Washa' narrated from Muhammad ibn Yahya from the executor of Ali ibn Al-Sari, who said:
I said to Abu Al-Hasan (as): "Ali ibn Al-Sari has passed away and appointed me as his executor."
Imam (as) said: "May Allah (swt) have mercy on him."
I said: "His son Ja'far committed an act with his father's umm walad (concubine), and he instructed me to exclude him from the inheritance."
Imam (as) said: "Exclude him if you are truthful, and he will suffer affliction."
He (the executor) said: I returned, and Ja'far brought me before Abu Yusuf the judge. Ja'far said to him, "May Allah (swt) rectify your affairs. I am Ja'far, the son of Ali ibn Al-Sari, and this is my father's executor. Order him to give me my inheritance from my father."
The judge asked me: "What do you say?"
I replied: "Yes, this is Ja'far, the son of Ali ibn Al-Sari, and I am his executor."
The judge said: "Then hand over his wealth to him."
I said: "I wish to speak with you."
He (the judge) said: "Come closer."
I approached him so that no one could hear me, and I said: "This man committed an act with his father's umm walad, and his father commanded me in his will to exclude him from the inheritance and not give him anything. I went to Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), in Medina, informed him, and sought his guidance. Imam (as) ordered me to exclude him from the inheritance and not give him anything."
The judge said: "By Allah (swt), did Abu Al-Hasan command you to do this?"
I said: "Yes."
He (the judge) made me swear three times and then said: "Carry out what you were ordered. His (as) word is decisive."
The executor said: After that, Ja'far was afflicted with madness.
Abu Muhammad Al-Hasan ibn Ali Al-Washa' said: I saw him after that.
records.arabicText: 5515 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْوَشَّاءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنْ وَصِيِّ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلسَّرِيِّ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنَّ عَلِيَّ بْنَ اَلسَّرِيِّ تُوُفِّيَ وَ أَوْصَى إِلَيَّ فَقَالَ "رَحِمَهُ اَللَّهُ" قُلْتُ وَ إِنَّ اِبْنَهُ جَعْفَراً وَقَعَ عَلَى أُمِّ وَلَدٍ لَهُ فَأَمَرَنِي أَنْ أُخْرِجَهُ مِنَ اَلْمِيرَاثِ فَقَالَ لِي "أَخْرِجْهُ إِنْ كُنْتَ صَادِقاً فَسَيُصِيبُهُ خَبَلٌ " قَالَ فَرَجَعْتُ فَقَدَّمَنِي إِلَى أَبِي يُوسُفَ اَلْقَاضِي فَقَالَ لَهُ أَصْلَحَكَ اَللَّهُ أَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلسَّرِيِّ وَ هَذَا وَصِيُّ أَبِي فَمُرْهُ أَنْ يَدْفَعَ إِلَيَّ مِيرَاثِي مِنْ أَبِي فَقَالَ لِي مَا تَقُولُ فَقُلْتُ نَعَمْ هَذَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلسَّرِيِّ وَ أَنَا وَصِيُّ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلسَّرِيِّ قَالَ فَادْفَعْ إِلَيْهِ مَالَهُ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ أُرِيدُ أَنْ أُكَلِّمَكَ قَالَ فَادْنُ مِنِّي فَدَنَوْتُ حَيْثُ لاَ يَسْمَعُ أَحَدٌ كَلاَمِي فَقُلْتُ لَهُ هَذَا وَقَعَ عَلَى أُمِّ وَلَدٍ لِأَبِيهِ فَأَمَرَنِي أَبُوهُ وَ أَوْصَى إِلَيَّ أَنْ أُخْرِجَهُ مِنَ اَلْمِيرَاثِ وَ لاَ أُوَرِّثَهُ شَيْئاً فَأَتَيْتُ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ بِالْمَدِينَةِ فَأَخْبَرْتُهُ وَ سَأَلْتُهُ فَأَمَرَنِي أَنْ أُخْرِجَهُ مِنَ اَلْمِيرَاثِ وَ لاَ أُوَرِّثَهُ شَيْئاً فَقَالَ اَللَّهَ إِنَّ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ أَمَرَكَ فَقُلْتُ نَعَمْ فَاسْتَحْلَفَنِي ثَلاَثاً ثُمَّ قَالَ لِي أَنْفِذْ مَا أَمَرَكَ فَالْقَوْلُ قَوْلُهُ قَالَ اَلْوَصِيُّ فَأَصَابَهُ اَلْخَبَلُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ أَبُو مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْوَشَّاءُ رَأَيْتُهُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5515 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali Al-Washa' narrated from Muhammad ibn Yahya from the executor of Ali ibn Al-Sari, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Al-Hasan (as): "Ali ibn Al-Sari has passed away and appointed me as his executor."
Imam (as) said: "May Allah (swt) have mercy on him."
I said: "His son Ja'far committed an act with his father's umm walad (concubine), and he instructed me to exclude him from the inheritance."
Imam (as) said: "Exclude him if you are truthful, and he will suffer affliction."
He (the executor) said: I returned, and Ja'far brought me before Abu Yusuf the judge. Ja'far said to him, "May Allah (swt) rectify your affairs. I am Ja'far, the son of Ali ibn Al-Sari, and this is my father's executor. Order him to give me my inheritance from my father."
The judge asked me: "What do you say?"
I replied: "Yes, this is Ja'far, the son of Ali ibn Al-Sari, and I am his executor."
The judge said: "Then hand over his wealth to him."
I said: "I wish to speak with you."
He (the judge) said: "Come closer."
I approached him so that no one could hear me, and I said: "This man committed an act with his father's umm walad, and his father commanded me in his will to exclude him from the inheritance and not give him anything. I went to Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), in Medina, informed him, and sought his guidance. Imam (as) ordered me to exclude him from the inheritance and not give him anything."
The judge said: "By Allah (swt), did Abu Al-Hasan command you to do this?"
I said: "Yes."
He (the judge) made me swear three times and then said: "Carry out what you were ordered. His (as) word is decisive."
The executor said: After that, Ja'far was afflicted with madness.
Abu Muhammad Al-Hasan ibn Ali Al-Washa' said: I saw him after that.

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Disinheriting His Son for Having Relations with His father's Umm Al-Walad
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5516 - And the confirmation of this is what was narrated by Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Isa from Abdul Aziz ibn Al-Muhtadi from Sa'id ibn Sa'd, who said:
'I asked Imam (as) - meaning Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) - about a man who had a son whom he acknowledged and then later disowned, excluding him from the inheritance. I am his executor, so what should I do?'
Imam (as) said: "The son remains legally his due to his prior public acknowledgment. The executor cannot deny him anything that is already known."
records.arabicText: 5516 - وَ تَصْدِيقُ ذَلِكَ مَا رَوَاهُ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلْعَزِيزِ بْنِ اَلْمُهْتَدِي عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ يَعْنِي أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ كَانَ لَهُ اِبْنٌ يَدَّعِيهِ فَنَفَاهُ وَ أَخْرَجَهُ مِنَ اَلْمِيرَاثِ وَ أَنَا وَصِيُّهُ فَكَيْفَ أَصْنَعُ فَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "لَزِمَهُ اَلْوَلَدُ لِإِقْرَارِهِ بِالْمَشْهَدِ لاَ يَدْفَعْهُ اَلْوَصِيُّ عَنْ شَيْءٍ قَدْ عَلِمَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5516 - And the confirmation of this is what was narrated by Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Isa from Abdul Aziz ibn Al-Muhtadi from Sa'id ibn Sa'd, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
'I asked Imam (as) - meaning Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) - about a man who had a son whom he acknowledged and then later disowned, excluding him from the inheritance. I am his executor, so what should I do?'
Imam (as) said: "The son remains legally his due to his prior public acknowledgment. The executor cannot deny him anything that is already known."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Cessation of an orphan's Orphanhood
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5517 - Mansur ibn Hazim narrated from Hisham from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"The end of an orphan's state of orphanhood is marked by reaching puberty, and that is when he reaches his full strength. However, if he reaches puberty but no sign of maturity is observed in him, and he is foolish or weak-minded, then his guardian should withhold his wealth from him."
records.arabicText: 5517 - رَوَى مَنْصُورُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ عَنْ هِشَامٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "اِنْقِطَاعُ يُتْمِ اَلْيَتِيمِ اَلاِحْتِلاَمُ وَ هُوَ أَشُدُّهُ وَ إِنِ اِحْتَلَمَ وَ لَمْ يُؤْنَسْ مِنْهُ رُشْدُهُ وَ كَانَ سَفِيهاً أَوْ ضَعِيفاً فَلْيُمْسِكْ عَنْهُ وَلِيُّهُ مَالَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5517 - Mansur ibn Hazim narrated from Hisham from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The end of an orphan's state of orphanhood is marked by reaching puberty, and that is when he reaches his full strength. However, if he reaches puberty but no sign of maturity is observed in him, and he is foolish or weak-minded, then his guardian should withhold his wealth from him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Cessation of an orphan's Orphanhood
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5518 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Mathanna ibn Rashid from Abu Basir from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about an orphan who had memorized the Qur'an and had no deficiency in his intellect. The one holding his wealth wishes to invest it until the orphan reaches puberty and then hand over his wealth to him.
Imam (as) said: "Even if he reaches puberty but does not have sound intellect, nothing should ever be handed over to him."
records.arabicText: 5518 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ مُثَنَّى بْنِ رَاشِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ يَتِيمٍ قَدْ قَرَأَ اَلْقُرْآنَ وَ لَيْسَ بِعَقْلِهِ بَأْسٌ وَ لَهُ مَالٌ عَلَى يَدَيْ رَجُلٍ فَأَرَادَ اَلَّذِي عِنْدَهُ اَلْمَالُ أَنْ يَعْمَلَ بِهِ حَتَّى يَحْتَلِمَ وَ يَدْفَعَ إِلَيْهِ مَالَهُ قَالَ "وَ إِنِ اِحْتَلَمَ وَ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ عَقْلٌ لَمْ يُدْفَعْ إِلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ أَبَداً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5518 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Mathanna ibn Rashid from Abu Basir from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about an orphan who had memorized the Qur'an and had no deficiency in his intellect. The one holding his wealth wishes to invest it until the orphan reaches puberty and then hand over his wealth to him.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Even if he reaches puberty but does not have sound intellect, nothing should ever be handed over to him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Cessation of an orphan's Orphanhood
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5519 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali Al-Washa' narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: "When a boy reaches the age of thirteen and enters into his fourteenth year, what is obligatory upon those who have reached puberty becomes obligatory upon him, whether he experiences a wet dream or not. His bad deeds are recorded against him, and his good deeds are recorded for him. Everything becomes permissible for him except if he is weak-minded or foolish."
records.arabicText: 5519 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْوَشَّاءُ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِذَا بَلَغَ اَلْغُلاَمُ أَشُدَّهُ ثَلاَثَ عَشْرَةَ سَنَةً وَ دَخَلَ فِي اَلْأَرْبَعَ عَشْرَةَ سَنَةً وَجَبَ عَلَيْهِ مَا وَجَبَ عَلَى اَلْمُحْتَلِمِينَ اِحْتَلَمَ أَوْ لَمْ يَحْتَلِمْ وَ كُتِبَتْ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّيِّئَاتُ وَ كُتِبَتْ لَهُ اَلْحَسَنَاتُ وَ جَازَ لَهُ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ ضَعِيفاً أَوْ سَفِيهاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5519 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali Al-Washa' narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When a boy reaches the age of thirteen and enters into his fourteenth year, what is obligatory upon those who have reached puberty becomes obligatory upon him, whether he experiences a wet dream or not. His bad deeds are recorded against him, and his good deeds are recorded for him. Everything becomes permissible for him except if he is weak-minded or foolish."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Cessation of an orphan's Orphanhood
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5520 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from 'Is ibn Al-Qasim from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about an orphan girl - when should her wealth be handed over to her?
Imam (as) said: "When you know that she will neither corrupt it nor waste it."
I then asked Imam (as): "What if she has gotten married?"
Imam (as) said: "Once she marries, the authority of the guardian over her ends."
COMPEV
قَالَ مُصَنِّفُ هَذَا الْكِتَابِ رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ إِذَا بَلَغَتْ تِسْعَ سِنِينَ.
The author of this book, may Allah (swt) have mercy on him, commented: This means when she reaches the age of nine years.
records.arabicText: 5520 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ عِيصِ بْنِ اَلْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْيَتِيمَةِ مَتَى يُدْفَعُ إِلَيْهَا مَالُهَا قَالَ "إِذَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّهَا لاَ تُفْسِدُ وَ لاَ تُضَيِّعُ" فَسَأَلْتُهُ إِنْ كَانَتْ قَدْ تَزَوَّجَتْ فَقَالَ "إِذَا تَزَوَّجَتْ فَقَدِ اِنْقَطَعَ مُلْكُ اَلْوَصِيِّ عَنْهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5520 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from 'Is ibn Al-Qasim from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about an orphan girl - when should her wealth be handed over to her?
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When you know that she will neither corrupt it nor waste it."
I then asked Imam (as): "What if she has gotten married?"
Imam (as) said: "Once she marries, the authority of the guardian over her ends."
COMPEV
قَالَ مُصَنِّفُ هَذَا الْكِتَابِ رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ إِذَا بَلَغَتْ تِسْعَ سِنِينَ.
The author of this book, may Allah (swt) have mercy on him, commented: This means when she reaches the age of nine years.

records.chapter: Chapter on the Cessation of an orphan's Orphanhood
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5521 - Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Zurara from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said: "A girl should not be consummated in marriage until she reaches nine or ten years of age."
records.arabicText: 5521 - وَ رَوَى مُوسَى بْنُ بَكْرٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ يُدْخَلُ بِالْجَارِيَةِ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ لَهَا تِسْعُ سِنِينَ أَوْ عَشْرٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5521 - Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Zurara from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A girl should not be consummated in marriage until she reaches nine or ten years of age."
